articles.headline: India-Canada Diplomatic Tensions Escalate as Diplomats Recalled, Expelled
articles.explainer:
• India recalls High Commissioner Sanjay Kumar Verma and other diplomats from Canada.
• Move follows Ottawa's investigation into Indian diplomats over the 2023 murder of Sikh separatist Hardeep Singh Nijjar.
• India orders the expulsion of Canada's acting High Commissioner Stewart Ross Wheeler and five other diplomats.
• Bilateral relations between the two nations reach a new low amidst escalating accusations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Sanjay Kumar Verma: India's High Commissioner to Canada recalled.
Stewart Ross Wheeler: Canada's acting High Commissioner to India expelled.
Hardeep Singh Nijjar: Sikh separatist whose murder in 2023 is central to the dispute.
Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations: Governs diplomatic immunity and expulsion.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the India-Canada diplomatic row on India's foreign policy objectives and global image.
Discuss the challenges of managing bilateral relations with countries hosting significant Indian diaspora populations with differing political views.
Examine the role of intelligence agencies and international law in cross-border security concerns and diplomatic disputes.
Evaluate the potential economic and strategic consequences of strained relations between India and Canada.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Canada, partially obscured, symbolizing diplomatic tension.
articles.trivia: Diplomatic immunity, enshrined in the Vienna Convention, protects diplomats from prosecution under the laws of the host country, but does not exempt them from being declared 'persona non grata' and expelled.

articles.headline: Centre Launches 'National Urban Green Spaces Mission' to Enhance City Ecosystems
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launches a new mission to boost green cover in Indian cities.
• Aims to integrate urban planning with ecological conservation, focusing on biodiversity and climate resilience.
• Emphasizes community participation, public-private partnerships, and use of native plant species.
• Seeks to improve air quality, reduce urban heat island effect, and enhance citizen well-being.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Mission Name: National Urban Green Spaces Mission.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Key Objectives: Increase urban green cover, biodiversity conservation, climate resilience, community participation.
Benefits: Improved air quality, reduced heat island effect, enhanced well-being.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of urban green spaces in mitigating the impacts of climate change and improving urban liveability.
Analyze the challenges in implementing urban greening initiatives in densely populated Indian cities and suggest policy measures.
Evaluate the role of public participation and local self-governance in the success of such environmental missions.
Examine how the mission aligns with India's broader sustainable development goals and international commitments.
articles.imageDescription: A lush green urban park with modern buildings in the background, symbolizing urban greening.
articles.trivia: Singapore, often called a 'City in a Garden', is renowned for its extensive urban greening initiatives, integrating nature into its urban fabric.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Data Privacy Rights in Landmark Ruling on State Surveillance
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivers a crucial judgment clarifying limits on state access to personal data.
• The ruling emphasizes the principles of necessity and proportionality for any state surveillance.
• Upholds the fundamental right to privacy, reiterating its importance in the digital age.
• Directs the government to review existing surveillance laws to ensure compliance with constitutional safeguards.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Fundamental Rights
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Right to Privacy: Declared a fundamental right under Article 21 (K.S. Puttaswamy judgment).
Principles for surveillance: Necessity and Proportionality.
Affected areas: Digital surveillance laws, personal data protection.
Constitutional safeguards: Emphasized by the Supreme Court.
mainsPoints: Discuss the delicate balance between national security interests and individual privacy rights in the context of state surveillance.
Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on India's digital governance framework and upcoming data protection legislation.
Examine how technological advancements challenge existing legal frameworks for privacy and the need for robust regulatory mechanisms.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in protecting fundamental rights against potential state overreach in the digital era.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents, with a digital padlock icon superimposed, symbolizing judicial oversight on data privacy.
articles.trivia: The landmark K.S. Puttaswamy judgment in 2017 unanimously declared the right to privacy a fundamental right under the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: India's Q2 GDP Growth Exceeds Expectations, Driven by Manufacturing and Services
articles.explainer:
• The National Statistical Office (NSO) releases provisional estimates for India's Q2 (July-September) GDP growth.
• Economic expansion surpasses analyst forecasts, indicating robust domestic demand.
• Manufacturing sector shows strong recovery, contributing significantly to the growth.
• Services sector continues its resilient performance, bolstering overall economic activity.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Growth, Development and Employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: Q2 GDP: Refers to the July-September quarter.
Estimates released by: National Statistical Office (NSO) under MoSPI.
Key drivers of growth: Manufacturing and Services sectors.
Significance: Indicates economic resilience amidst global uncertainties.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's robust GDP growth in the current quarter and its implications for fiscal policy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for sustaining this growth trajectory, particularly in the context of global economic headwinds.
Evaluate the role of government policies and reforms in boosting key sectors like manufacturing and services.
Examine the potential impact of this economic performance on employment generation and poverty reduction efforts in India.
articles.imageDescription: A rising graph superimposed on a map of India, symbolizing economic growth.
articles.trivia: India's economy is the fifth-largest in the world by nominal GDP and the third-largest by purchasing power parity (PPP).

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Disaster Management
articles.explainer:
• ISRO's PSLV-C59 rocket successfully places an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) into orbit.
• The satellite is equipped with high-resolution imaging capabilities for precise data collection.
• Primarily designed to enhance India's disaster management capabilities, including flood and cyclone monitoring.
• Reinforces India's commitment to utilizing space technology for societal benefits and national development.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
prelimsPoints: Launch Vehicle: PSLV-C59 (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle).
Satellite Type: Earth Observation Satellite (EOS).
Primary Application: Disaster management (flood, cyclone, agricultural monitoring).
Key capabilities: High-resolution imaging, data collection.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of space technology and Earth Observation Satellites in enhancing India's disaster preparedness and response mechanisms.
Evaluate ISRO's contributions to India's socio-economic development through its various satellite programs.
Examine the strategic importance of indigenous space capabilities for national security and technological self-reliance.
Analyze the potential of geospatial data in urban planning, resource management, and environmental monitoring in India.
articles.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space from a launchpad, with a satellite orbiting Earth in the background.
articles.trivia: The PSLV is known as ISRO's 'workhorse' launch vehicle, having successfully launched numerous Indian and international satellites.

articles.headline: India Hosts Inaugural India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue in New Delhi
articles.explainer:
• India initiates the first-ever India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue in the capital.
• The dialogue focuses on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region to address maritime challenges.
• Key discussions include combating piracy, illegal fishing, and promoting a rules-based maritime order.
• Aims to strengthen bilateral and regional ties, aligning with India's Act East Policy and Indo-Pacific vision.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Dialogue Name: India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue (inaugural).
Location: New Delhi.
Participants: India and ASEAN member states.
Key Focus Areas: Maritime security, piracy, illegal fishing, rules-based order in Indo-Pacific.
Policy Alignment: India's Act East Policy, Indo-Pacific vision.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN in the evolving geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss the common maritime challenges faced by India and ASEAN countries and the potential for collaborative solutions.
Evaluate how this dialogue contributes to India's Act East Policy and its broader vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Examine the implications of enhanced maritime cooperation on regional trade, connectivity, and stability.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from different countries sailing in formation, symbolizing maritime cooperation.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was founded in 1967 by Indonesia, Malaysia, the Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand, and now includes ten member states.

articles.headline: Government Unveils National Policy on Lithium-ion Battery Recycling to Boost Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change introduces a comprehensive policy for lithium-ion battery recycling.
• Aims to address the growing e-waste challenge and promote sustainable resource management.
• The policy incentivizes establishing robust recycling infrastructure and technological advancements.
• Seeks to reduce India's reliance on imported critical minerals and foster a circular economy approach.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Policy Name: National Policy on Lithium-ion Battery Recycling.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Objectives: E-waste management, sustainable resource use, reduce import dependency, circular economy.
Importance: Growing use of Li-ion batteries in EVs, electronics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the environmental and economic imperatives behind India's new policy on lithium-ion battery recycling.
Analyze the challenges in establishing a comprehensive recycling ecosystem for Li-ion batteries in India, including technological and logistical hurdles.
Evaluate how the circular economy principles embedded in the policy can contribute to India's resource security and climate goals.
Examine the potential of this policy to catalyze green job creation and foster innovation in battery recycling technologies.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the lifecycle of a battery, from manufacturing to recycling, highlighting circularity.
articles.trivia: Lithium-ion batteries are widely used in electric vehicles (EVs) and portable electronic devices due to their high energy density.

articles.headline: India Leads Global Discussions on Digital Public Infrastructure Implementation Post-G20 Summit
articles.explainer:
• Following the G20 New Delhi Declaration, India spearheads global conversations on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• Focuses on scaling India's successful DPI model (e.g., Aadhaar, UPI) to other developing nations.
• Discussions center on interoperability, inclusive access, and governance frameworks for DPI.
• Ministry of Electronics and IT plays a crucial role in promoting India's technological leadership and digital diplomacy.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: G20 New Delhi Declaration: Highlighted DPI as a key area of cooperation.
India's DPI examples: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker, CoWIN.
Key aspects of DPI: Interoperability, inclusive access, open standards.
Ministry involved: Ministry of Electronics and IT (MeitY).
mainsPoints: Discuss how India's leadership in Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) can contribute to global digital inclusion and sustainable development, particularly in the Global South.
Analyze the key features of India's DPI model and its potential for replication in other countries, considering diverse socio-economic contexts.
Examine the challenges and opportunities associated with promoting global interoperability and governance standards for DPI.
Evaluate the strategic importance of digital diplomacy for India in enhancing its global influence and fostering technological partnerships.
articles.imageDescription: A world map with interconnected digital lines, highlighting India, symbolizing global digital connectivity.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023, showcasing its massive scale and efficiency.

articles.headline: New Wildlife Sanctuary Declared in Western Ghats to Protect Endemic Species
articles.explainer:
• A new wildlife sanctuary is established in a critical biodiversity hotspot within the Western Ghats.
• The declaration aims to provide enhanced protection for several endemic and endangered species of flora and fauna.
• This initiative is part of India's broader commitment to biodiversity conservation and ecological preservation.
• Expected to boost eco-tourism and research opportunities while involving local communities in conservation efforts.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Location: Western Ghats (a UNESCO World Heritage Site and biodiversity hotspot).
Purpose: Protection of endemic and endangered flora and fauna.
Conservation status: Wildlife Sanctuary.
Benefits: Biodiversity preservation, eco-tourism, research.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the effectiveness of declaring wildlife sanctuaries and other protected areas in conserving biodiversity in India.
Examine the challenges of balancing conservation efforts with the developmental needs and livelihoods of local communities.
Evaluate the role of government policies and international agreements in protecting India's natural heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with dense forest and a diverse array of wildlife.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Releases Progress Report on NEP 2020 Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education publishes an annual progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlights significant achievements in foundational literacy and numeracy programs.
• Identifies ongoing challenges, particularly in teacher training and infrastructure development.
• Emphasizes the integration of skill development and vocational education across all levels.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Policy: National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Education.
Key achievements: Foundational literacy, early childhood care.
Challenges: Teacher training, digital infrastructure, funding.
Focus areas: Skill development, multidisciplinary approach.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in reshaping India's education landscape and achieving inclusive growth.
Analyze the major successes and persistent challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020, particularly at the school education level.
Examine the critical role of teacher training and capacity building in realizing the objectives of the new education policy.
Evaluate how NEP 2020 addresses the skill gap and prepares the Indian workforce for the demands of the 21st century.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, engaged in learning with digital tools.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India, after policies in 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: India and Japan Hold Strategic Dialogue on Cooperation in Critical and Emerging Technologies
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan conduct a high-level strategic dialogue focusing on collaboration in critical technologies.
• Key areas of discussion include semiconductors, artificial intelligence (AI), and quantum computing.
• Aims to build resilient and diversified supply chains, reducing dependence on single sources.
• Reinforces the bilateral strategic partnership and shared commitment to technological innovation and security.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: Countries involved: India and Japan.
Focus areas: Semiconductors, AI, Quantum Computing, 5G/6G.
Objectives: Resilient supply chains, technological security, strategic partnership.
Significance: Enhances cooperation in advanced tech sectors.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic rationale behind India's enhanced cooperation with Japan in critical and emerging technologies.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India in developing a robust semiconductor ecosystem and reducing import dependence.
Examine how collaboration in AI and quantum computing can contribute to India's economic growth and national security.
Evaluate the role of such bilateral dialogues in shaping global technology governance and standards.
articles.imageDescription: Intertwined flags of India and Japan, with digital circuits in the background, symbolizing technological partnership.
articles.trivia: Japan is a leading nation in robotics and advanced manufacturing, making it a natural partner for India's technological ambitions.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches New Scheme to Boost Rural and Eco-Tourism in Lesser-Known Regions
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism introduces a new scheme aimed at promoting rural and eco-tourism across India.
• Focuses on developing tourism infrastructure and experiences in offbeat and lesser-known destinations.
• Seeks to empower local communities through skill development and employment generation.
• Emphasizes sustainable tourism practices, cultural preservation, and showcasing natural beauty.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: Scheme Aim: Promote rural and eco-tourism.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Tourism.
Key objectives: Local empowerment, employment, skill development, sustainable practices.
Benefits: Cultural preservation, showcasing natural beauty, inclusive growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of rural and eco-tourism in fostering inclusive growth and sustainable development in India's remote regions.
Analyze the challenges faced by rural tourism initiatives, including infrastructure deficits and marketing hurdles, and propose solutions.
Examine how community participation and local ownership can be leveraged to ensure the long-term success and sustainability of such tourism projects.
Evaluate the role of tourism in preserving indigenous cultures and traditions while promoting economic opportunities.
articles.imageDescription: A picturesque rural landscape with traditional homes and tourists engaging with local culture.
articles.trivia: India has a vast potential for rural tourism due to its diverse geography, rich cultural heritage, and traditional village life.

articles.headline: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Significant Progress in Pilot Projects
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy reports substantial advancements in pilot projects under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• Pilot projects demonstrate technical and economic viability of green hydrogen production and application across various sectors.
• Aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• Contributes significantly to reducing carbon emissions and enhancing energy security.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Infrastructure: Energy
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Mission: National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
Objective: Make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Process: Green hydrogen produced via electrolysis using renewable energy.
Benefits: Decarbonization, energy security, industrial feedstock.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the technological and economic challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption across various sectors in India.
Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors and contribute to India's net-zero targets.
Examine the policy support and infrastructure development required to establish India as a global leader in the hydrogen economy.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, emitting no greenhouse gases.

articles.headline: Government Constitutes High-Level Task Force to Review India's Cyber Security Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) establishes a high-level task force to conduct a comprehensive review of India's cybersecurity framework.
• The task force is mandated to address evolving cyber threats, including state-sponsored attacks and cyber warfare.
• Key recommendations focus on strengthening critical information infrastructure protection and capacity building.
• Emphasizes international cooperation and public-private partnerships to enhance national cyber resilience.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Task Force: High-level for cybersecurity review.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA).
Focus: Evolving cyber threats, critical infrastructure protection, capacity building.
Recommendations: International cooperation, public-private partnerships.
Related agency: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team).
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges posed by evolving cyber threats to India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key recommendations of the task force and their potential impact on strengthening India's cybersecurity posture.
Examine the role of international cooperation and public-private partnerships in building a robust and resilient cybersecurity framework.
Evaluate the importance of a comprehensive national cybersecurity strategy in protecting critical information infrastructure and citizen data.
articles.imageDescription: A digital shield icon overlaying a network of interconnected lines, symbolizing cybersecurity protection.
articles.trivia: India's Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) is the national agency for handling cyber security incidents.

articles.headline: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs Releases Urban Mobility Index 2024, Highlighting Key Performance Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveils the Urban Mobility Index (UMI) 2024 for Indian cities.
• The index evaluates cities based on parameters such as public transport availability, infrastructure quality, and sustainability.
• Aims to benchmark urban mobility performance, identify areas for improvement, and promote best practices.
• Encourages cities to adopt smart mobility solutions and integrate transport planning with urban development.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Index Name: Urban Mobility Index (UMI) 2024.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Parameters: Public transport, infrastructure, sustainability, accessibility.
Objective: Benchmarking, promoting best practices, smart mobility.
Significance: Guides urban transport planning.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical challenges faced by urban transport systems in India's rapidly growing cities.
Analyze how an Urban Mobility Index can serve as a tool for improving urban planning and promoting sustainable transport solutions.
Examine the role of technology and smart city initiatives in enhancing urban mobility and last-mile connectivity.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits of efficient and accessible public transport for urban populations.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized city skyline with various modes of transport (bus, metro, bicycle) moving efficiently.
articles.trivia: Delhi Metro, one of the largest and busiest metro networks in the world, is a prime example of a successful urban mobility project in India.

articles.headline: India and Sri Lanka Sign Economic Cooperation Pact, Boosting Connectivity and Trade
articles.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka ink a new economic cooperation agreement aimed at strengthening bilateral ties.
• The pact focuses on enhancing connectivity projects, including port development and ferry services.
• Aims to boost trade, investment, and tourism between the two South Asian nations.
• Underscores India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and commitment to regional stability and prosperity.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Countries: India and Sri Lanka.
Agreement: Economic Cooperation Pact.
Focus: Connectivity (ports, ferry), trade, investment, tourism.
Policy: India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy.
Strategic Importance: Indian Ocean region.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the new economic cooperation pact between India and Sri Lanka for regional stability and India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy.
Analyze the potential economic benefits of enhanced connectivity and trade for both nations, particularly for Sri Lanka's post-crisis recovery.
Examine the challenges and geopolitical considerations that influence India-Sri Lanka relations in the Indian Ocean region.
Evaluate the role of economic diplomacy in strengthening India's strategic partnerships with its immediate neighbors.
articles.imageDescription: A handshake between two individuals, with the flags of India and Sri Lanka in the background, symbolizing cooperation.
articles.trivia: The Palk Strait, a narrow strip of water, separates India (Tamil Nadu) and Sri Lanka, and is a historic trade route.

articles.headline: Progress on National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems Reviewed
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology reviews the progress of the National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS).
• The mission focuses on developing technologies like AI, IoT, Robotics, and Quantum Computing.
• Aims to create a strong ecosystem for R&D and human resource development in CPS technologies.
• Crucial for India's technological self-reliance and competitiveness in the Fourth Industrial Revolution.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Mission Name: National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS).
Nodal Department: Department of Science & Technology (DST).
Technologies covered: AI, IoT, Robotics, Quantum Computing, Big Data Analytics.
Objective: R&D, human resource development, technological self-reliance.
Significance: Aligns with Industry 4.0.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) in driving India's economic growth and societal development.
Analyze the role of the National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS) in fostering indigenous innovation and human resource capabilities.
Examine the ethical and governance challenges associated with the widespread adoption of AI, IoT, and robotics in various sectors.
Evaluate how India can leverage CPS technologies to enhance its competitiveness in the global technological landscape and prepare for the Fourth Industrial Revolution.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic cityscape with interconnected smart devices and robots, representing Cyber-Physical Systems.
articles.trivia: Cyber-Physical Systems are integrations of computation, networking, and physical processes, often seen in smart grids, autonomous vehicles, and advanced manufacturing.

highlights: India-Canada diplomatic tensions escalate with mutual recall and expulsion of diplomats over Nijjar murder probe.
Centre launches 'National Urban Green Spaces Mission' to enhance city ecosystems and climate resilience.
Supreme Court reinforces data privacy rights, setting limits on state surveillance with necessity and proportionality principles.
India's Q2 GDP growth surpasses expectations, driven by strong performance in manufacturing and services sectors.
ISRO successfully launches advanced Earth Observation Satellite to bolster disaster management capabilities.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent diplomatic tensions between India and Canada, consider the following statements:
1. India recalled its High Commissioner Sanjay Kumar Verma in response to Canadian investigations.
2. Canada's acting High Commissioner Stewart Ross Wheeler was among the diplomats expelled by India.
3. The dispute primarily concerns allegations related to the murder of Hardeep Singh Nijjar.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. India recalled its High Commissioner Sanjay Kumar Verma, and expelled Canada's acting High Commissioner Stewart Ross Wheeler and other diplomats. The core of the diplomatic row is Canada's investigation into the murder of Sikh separatist Hardeep Singh Nijjar in 2023.

prelimsMocks.question:
The 'National Urban Green Spaces Mission' recently launched by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs aims to achieve which of the following?
1. Increase green cover in urban areas.
2. Promote biodiversity conservation within cities.
3. Reduce urban heat island effect.
4. Facilitate public-private partnerships for urban infrastructure.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Urban Green Spaces Mission aims to increase urban green cover, promote biodiversity, reduce the urban heat island effect, and encourage community/public-private participation for sustainable urban ecosystems. All the listed objectives are part of the mission.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following principles were emphasized by the Supreme Court in its recent ruling on data privacy and state surveillance?
1. Doctrine of Basic Structure
2. Principle of Necessity
3. Principle of Proportionality
4. Doctrine of Stare Decisis
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court's ruling on data privacy and state surveillance particularly emphasized the principles of necessity and proportionality, stating that any state action impacting privacy must be necessary for a legitimate aim and proportionate to that aim. While 'Doctrine of Basic Structure' and 'Doctrine of Stare Decisis' are constitutional principles, they were not the specific emphasis of this particular ruling.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's National Green Hydrogen Mission:
1. Its primary goal is to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using electricity generated from fossil fuels.
3. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is the nodal ministry for this mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the mission aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen. Statement 2 is incorrect because green hydrogen is produced using renewable electricity (e.g., solar, wind) for electrolysis, not fossil fuels. Statement 3 is correct, as the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is indeed the nodal ministry.

prelimsMocks.question:
The inaugural India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue recently held in New Delhi focused on which of the following?
1. Enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
2. Combating piracy and illegal fishing.
3. Promoting a rules-based maritime order.
4. Discussing bilateral trade agreements with individual ASEAN members.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The inaugural India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue focused on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific, combating piracy and illegal fishing, and promoting a rules-based maritime order. While trade is a component of India-ASEAN relations, this specific dialogue was centered on maritime security, not bilateral trade agreements with individual members. Hence, statement 4 is not the primary focus of this specific dialogue.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the multifaceted implications of the escalating diplomatic tensions between India and Canada on India's foreign policy, diaspora relations, and global standing. Suggest measures India can take to navigate such complex international disputes.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighbourhood- relations; Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the India-Canada diplomatic row and its immediate triggers. Discuss the implications: foreign policy challenges (balancing national security with international norms), impact on Indian diaspora in Canada, potential strain on trade/investment, and India's global image. Suggest measures like calibrated diplomacy, strengthening intelligence cooperation, engaging with diaspora constructively, and emphasizing rule of law.

mainsMocks.question: Critically evaluate the potential of the 'National Urban Green Spaces Mission' to address environmental degradation and enhance quality of life in Indian cities. What are the key challenges in its implementation and how can they be overcome?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Urbanization, their problems and their remedies. GS1: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the mission's objectives (green cover, biodiversity, climate resilience). Discuss its potential benefits: improved air quality, reduced heat island effect, enhanced public health, aesthetic value. Identify challenges like land availability, funding, public participation, maintenance, encroachment, and coordination among agencies. Suggest solutions like innovative financing, policy integration, community engagement, and technological interventions.

mainsMocks.question: In light of the Supreme Court's recent ruling on data privacy and state surveillance, discuss the challenges of balancing national security interests with the fundamental right to privacy in the digital age. What legislative and institutional reforms are needed to ensure robust data protection in India?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Fundamental Rights; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Role of judiciary. GS3: Basics of cyber security.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the SC's emphasis on necessity and proportionality. Discuss the inherent tension between state's need for surveillance for national security and individual's fundamental right to privacy. Highlight challenges like evolving technology, lack of clear legal frameworks, potential for misuse. Propose reforms: comprehensive data protection law (e.g., DPDP Act), independent oversight mechanisms, judicial review, public awareness, and technological safeguards. Reference the K.S. Puttaswamy judgment.

quickBytes.text: India's Q2 GDP growth is driven by robust performance in manufacturing and services sectors.
The Western Ghats are one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
Green hydrogen production uses renewable electricity for electrolysis, leading to zero emissions.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 aims for holistic and multidisciplinary education.
Diplomatic immunity is governed by the Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations.
ISRO's PSLV is known as its 'workhorse' launch vehicle for satellites.
The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 by the Supreme Court.
Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in India includes Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
Circular economy principles aim to reduce waste and maximize resource utilization.
India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy guides its relations with immediate neighbors like Sri Lanka.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches New Scheme to Boost Rural and Eco-Tourism in Lesser-Known Regions
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism introduces a new scheme aimed at promoting rural and eco-tourism across India.
• Focuses on developing tourism infrastructure and experiences in offbeat and lesser-known destinations.
• Seeks to empower local communities through skill development and employment generation.
• Emphasizes sustainable tourism practices, cultural preservation, and showcasing natural beauty.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: Scheme Aim: Promote rural and eco-tourism.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Tourism.
Key objectives: Local empowerment, employment, skill development, sustainable practices.
Benefits: Cultural preservation, showcasing natural beauty, inclusive growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of rural and eco-tourism in fostering inclusive growth and sustainable development in India's remote regions.
Analyze the challenges faced by rural tourism initiatives, including infrastructure deficits and marketing hurdles, and propose solutions.
Examine how community participation and local ownership can be leveraged to ensure the long-term success and sustainability of such tourism projects.
Evaluate the role of tourism in preserving indigenous cultures and traditions while promoting economic opportunities.
GS1.imageDescription: A picturesque rural landscape with traditional homes and tourists engaging with local culture.
GS1.trivia: India has a vast potential for rural tourism due to its diverse geography, rich cultural heritage, and traditional village life.

GS2.headline: India-Canada Diplomatic Tensions Escalate as Diplomats Recalled, Expelled
GS2.explainer:
• India recalls High Commissioner Sanjay Kumar Verma and other diplomats from Canada.
• Move follows Ottawa's investigation into Indian diplomats over the 2023 murder of Sikh separatist Hardeep Singh Nijjar.
• India orders the expulsion of Canada's acting High Commissioner Stewart Ross Wheeler and five other diplomats.
• Bilateral relations between the two nations reach a new low amidst escalating accusations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Sanjay Kumar Verma: India's High Commissioner to Canada recalled.
Stewart Ross Wheeler: Canada's acting High Commissioner to India expelled.
Hardeep Singh Nijjar: Sikh separatist whose murder in 2023 is central to the dispute.
Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations: Governs diplomatic immunity and expulsion.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the India-Canada diplomatic row on India's foreign policy objectives and global image.
Discuss the challenges of managing bilateral relations with countries hosting significant Indian diaspora populations with differing political views.
Examine the role of intelligence agencies and international law in cross-border security concerns and diplomatic disputes.
Evaluate the potential economic and strategic consequences of strained relations between India and Canada.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Canada, partially obscured, symbolizing diplomatic tension.
GS2.trivia: Diplomatic immunity, enshrined in the Vienna Convention, protects diplomats from prosecution under the laws of the host country, but does not exempt them from being declared 'persona non grata' and expelled.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Data Privacy Rights in Landmark Ruling on State Surveillance
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivers a crucial judgment clarifying limits on state access to personal data.
• The ruling emphasizes the principles of necessity and proportionality for any state surveillance.
• Upholds the fundamental right to privacy, reiterating its importance in the digital age.
• Directs the government to review existing surveillance laws to ensure compliance with constitutional safeguards.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Fundamental Rights
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Right to Privacy: Declared a fundamental right under Article 21 (K.S. Puttaswamy judgment).
Principles for surveillance: Necessity and Proportionality.
Affected areas: Digital surveillance laws, personal data protection.
Constitutional safeguards: Emphasized by the Supreme Court.
mainsPoints: Discuss the delicate balance between national security interests and individual privacy rights in the context of state surveillance.
Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on India's digital governance framework and upcoming data protection legislation.
Examine how technological advancements challenge existing legal frameworks for privacy and the need for robust regulatory mechanisms.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in protecting fundamental rights against potential state overreach in the digital era.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents, with a digital padlock icon superimposed, symbolizing judicial oversight on data privacy.
GS2.trivia: The landmark K.S. Puttaswamy judgment in 2017 unanimously declared the right to privacy a fundamental right under the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: India Hosts Inaugural India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue in New Delhi
GS2.explainer:
• India initiates the first-ever India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue in the capital.
• The dialogue focuses on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region to address maritime challenges.
• Key discussions include combating piracy, illegal fishing, and promoting a rules-based maritime order.
• Aims to strengthen bilateral and regional ties, aligning with India's Act East Policy and Indo-Pacific vision.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Dialogue Name: India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue (inaugural).
Location: New Delhi.
Participants: India and ASEAN member states.
Key Focus Areas: Maritime security, piracy, illegal fishing, rules-based order in Indo-Pacific.
Policy Alignment: India's Act East Policy, Indo-Pacific vision.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN in the evolving geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss the common maritime challenges faced by India and ASEAN countries and the potential for collaborative solutions.
Evaluate how this dialogue contributes to India's Act East Policy and its broader vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Examine the implications of enhanced maritime cooperation on regional trade, connectivity, and stability.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships from different countries sailing in formation, symbolizing maritime cooperation.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was founded in 1967 by Indonesia, Malaysia, the Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand, and now includes ten member states.

GS2.headline: India Leads Global Discussions on Digital Public Infrastructure Implementation Post-G20 Summit
GS2.explainer:
• Following the G20 New Delhi Declaration, India spearheads global conversations on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• Focuses on scaling India's successful DPI model (e.g., Aadhaar, UPI) to other developing nations.
• Discussions center on interoperability, inclusive access, and governance frameworks for DPI.
• Ministry of Electronics and IT plays a crucial role in promoting India's technological leadership and digital diplomacy.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: G20 New Delhi Declaration: Highlighted DPI as a key area of cooperation.
India's DPI examples: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker, CoWIN.
Key aspects of DPI: Interoperability, inclusive access, open standards.
Ministry involved: Ministry of Electronics and IT (MeitY).
mainsPoints: Discuss how India's leadership in Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) can contribute to global digital inclusion and sustainable development, particularly in the Global South.
Analyze the key features of India's DPI model and its potential for replication in other countries, considering diverse socio-economic contexts.
Examine the challenges and opportunities associated with promoting global interoperability and governance standards for DPI.
Evaluate the strategic importance of digital diplomacy for India in enhancing its global influence and fostering technological partnerships.
GS2.imageDescription: A world map with interconnected digital lines, highlighting India, symbolizing global digital connectivity.
GS2.trivia: India's UPI system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023, showcasing its massive scale and efficiency.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Education Releases Progress Report on NEP 2020 Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education publishes an annual progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlights significant achievements in foundational literacy and numeracy programs.
• Identifies ongoing challenges, particularly in teacher training and infrastructure development.
• Emphasizes the integration of skill development and vocational education across all levels.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Policy: National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Education.
Key achievements: Foundational literacy, early childhood care.
Challenges: Teacher training, digital infrastructure, funding.
Focus areas: Skill development, multidisciplinary approach.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in reshaping India's education landscape and achieving inclusive growth.
Analyze the major successes and persistent challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020, particularly at the school education level.
Examine the critical role of teacher training and capacity building in realizing the objectives of the new education policy.
Evaluate how NEP 2020 addresses the skill gap and prepares the Indian workforce for the demands of the 21st century.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, engaged in learning with digital tools.
GS2.trivia: NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India, after policies in 1968 and 1986.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Hold Strategic Dialogue on Cooperation in Critical and Emerging Technologies
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan conduct a high-level strategic dialogue focusing on collaboration in critical technologies.
• Key areas of discussion include semiconductors, artificial intelligence (AI), and quantum computing.
• Aims to build resilient and diversified supply chains, reducing dependence on single sources.
• Reinforces the bilateral strategic partnership and shared commitment to technological innovation and security.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: Countries involved: India and Japan.
Focus areas: Semiconductors, AI, Quantum Computing, 5G/6G.
Objectives: Resilient supply chains, technological security, strategic partnership.
Significance: Enhances cooperation in advanced tech sectors.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic rationale behind India's enhanced cooperation with Japan in critical and emerging technologies.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India in developing a robust semiconductor ecosystem and reducing import dependence.
Examine how collaboration in AI and quantum computing can contribute to India's economic growth and national security.
Evaluate the role of such bilateral dialogues in shaping global technology governance and standards.
GS2.imageDescription: Intertwined flags of India and Japan, with digital circuits in the background, symbolizing technological partnership.
GS2.trivia: Japan is a leading nation in robotics and advanced manufacturing, making it a natural partner for India's technological ambitions.

GS2.headline: India and Sri Lanka Sign Economic Cooperation Pact, Boosting Connectivity and Trade
GS2.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka ink a new economic cooperation agreement aimed at strengthening bilateral ties.
• The pact focuses on enhancing connectivity projects, including port development and ferry services.
• Aims to boost trade, investment, and tourism between the two South Asian nations.
• Underscores India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and commitment to regional stability and prosperity.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Countries: India and Sri Lanka.
Agreement: Economic Cooperation Pact.
Focus: Connectivity (ports, ferry), trade, investment, tourism.
Policy: India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy.
Strategic Importance: Indian Ocean region.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the new economic cooperation pact between India and Sri Lanka for regional stability and India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy.
Analyze the potential economic benefits of enhanced connectivity and trade for both nations, particularly for Sri Lanka's post-crisis recovery.
Examine the challenges and geopolitical considerations that influence India-Sri Lanka relations in the Indian Ocean region.
Evaluate the role of economic diplomacy in strengthening India's strategic partnerships with its immediate neighbors.
GS2.imageDescription: A handshake between two individuals, with the flags of India and Sri Lanka in the background, symbolizing cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The Palk Strait, a narrow strip of water, separates India (Tamil Nadu) and Sri Lanka, and is a historic trade route.

GS3.headline: Centre Launches 'National Urban Green Spaces Mission' to Enhance City Ecosystems
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launches a new mission to boost green cover in Indian cities.
• Aims to integrate urban planning with ecological conservation, focusing on biodiversity and climate resilience.
• Emphasizes community participation, public-private partnerships, and use of native plant species.
• Seeks to improve air quality, reduce urban heat island effect, and enhance citizen well-being.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Mission Name: National Urban Green Spaces Mission.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Key Objectives: Increase urban green cover, biodiversity conservation, climate resilience, community participation.
Benefits: Improved air quality, reduced heat island effect, enhanced well-being.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of urban green spaces in mitigating the impacts of climate change and improving urban liveability.
Analyze the challenges in implementing urban greening initiatives in densely populated Indian cities and suggest policy measures.
Evaluate the role of public participation and local self-governance in the success of such environmental missions.
Examine how the mission aligns with India's broader sustainable development goals and international commitments.
GS3.imageDescription: A lush green urban park with modern buildings in the background, symbolizing urban greening.
GS3.trivia: Singapore, often called a 'City in a Garden', is renowned for its extensive urban greening initiatives, integrating nature into its urban fabric.

GS3.headline: India's Q2 GDP Growth Exceeds Expectations, Driven by Manufacturing and Services
GS3.explainer:
• The National Statistical Office (NSO) releases provisional estimates for India's Q2 (July-September) GDP growth.
• Economic expansion surpasses analyst forecasts, indicating robust domestic demand.
• Manufacturing sector shows strong recovery, contributing significantly to the growth.
• Services sector continues its resilient performance, bolstering overall economic activity.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Growth, Development and Employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: Q2 GDP: Refers to the July-September quarter.
Estimates released by: National Statistical Office (NSO) under MoSPI.
Key drivers of growth: Manufacturing and Services sectors.
Significance: Indicates economic resilience amidst global uncertainties.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's robust GDP growth in the current quarter and its implications for fiscal policy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for sustaining this growth trajectory, particularly in the context of global economic headwinds.
Evaluate the role of government policies and reforms in boosting key sectors like manufacturing and services.
Examine the potential impact of this economic performance on employment generation and poverty reduction efforts in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A rising graph superimposed on a map of India, symbolizing economic growth.
GS3.trivia: India's economy is the fifth-largest in the world by nominal GDP and the third-largest by purchasing power parity (PPP).

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Disaster Management
GS3.explainer:
• ISRO's PSLV-C59 rocket successfully places an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) into orbit.
• The satellite is equipped with high-resolution imaging capabilities for precise data collection.
• Primarily designed to enhance India's disaster management capabilities, including flood and cyclone monitoring.
• Reinforces India's commitment to utilizing space technology for societal benefits and national development.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
prelimsPoints: Launch Vehicle: PSLV-C59 (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle).
Satellite Type: Earth Observation Satellite (EOS).
Primary Application: Disaster management (flood, cyclone, agricultural monitoring).
Key capabilities: High-resolution imaging, data collection.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of space technology and Earth Observation Satellites in enhancing India's disaster preparedness and response mechanisms.
Evaluate ISRO's contributions to India's socio-economic development through its various satellite programs.
Examine the strategic importance of indigenous space capabilities for national security and technological self-reliance.
Analyze the potential of geospatial data in urban planning, resource management, and environmental monitoring in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space from a launchpad, with a satellite orbiting Earth in the background.
GS3.trivia: The PSLV is known as ISRO's 'workhorse' launch vehicle, having successfully launched numerous Indian and international satellites.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils National Policy on Lithium-ion Battery Recycling to Boost Circular Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change introduces a comprehensive policy for lithium-ion battery recycling.
• Aims to address the growing e-waste challenge and promote sustainable resource management.
• The policy incentivizes establishing robust recycling infrastructure and technological advancements.
• Seeks to reduce India's reliance on imported critical minerals and foster a circular economy approach.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Policy Name: National Policy on Lithium-ion Battery Recycling.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Objectives: E-waste management, sustainable resource use, reduce import dependency, circular economy.
Importance: Growing use of Li-ion batteries in EVs, electronics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the environmental and economic imperatives behind India's new policy on lithium-ion battery recycling.
Analyze the challenges in establishing a comprehensive recycling ecosystem for Li-ion batteries in India, including technological and logistical hurdles.
Evaluate how the circular economy principles embedded in the policy can contribute to India's resource security and climate goals.
Examine the potential of this policy to catalyze green job creation and foster innovation in battery recycling technologies.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the lifecycle of a battery, from manufacturing to recycling, highlighting circularity.
GS3.trivia: Lithium-ion batteries are widely used in electric vehicles (EVs) and portable electronic devices due to their high energy density.

GS3.headline: New Wildlife Sanctuary Declared in Western Ghats to Protect Endemic Species
GS3.explainer:
• A new wildlife sanctuary is established in a critical biodiversity hotspot within the Western Ghats.
• The declaration aims to provide enhanced protection for several endemic and endangered species of flora and fauna.
• This initiative is part of India's broader commitment to biodiversity conservation and ecological preservation.
• Expected to boost eco-tourism and research opportunities while involving local communities in conservation efforts.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Location: Western Ghats (a UNESCO World Heritage Site and biodiversity hotspot).
Purpose: Protection of endemic and endangered flora and fauna.
Conservation status: Wildlife Sanctuary.
Benefits: Biodiversity preservation, eco-tourism, research.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the effectiveness of declaring wildlife sanctuaries and other protected areas in conserving biodiversity in India.
Examine the challenges of balancing conservation efforts with the developmental needs and livelihoods of local communities.
Evaluate the role of government policies and international agreements in protecting India's natural heritage.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with dense forest and a diverse array of wildlife.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

GS3.headline: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Significant Progress in Pilot Projects
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy reports substantial advancements in pilot projects under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• Pilot projects demonstrate technical and economic viability of green hydrogen production and application across various sectors.
• Aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• Contributes significantly to reducing carbon emissions and enhancing energy security.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Infrastructure: Energy
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Mission: National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
Objective: Make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Process: Green hydrogen produced via electrolysis using renewable energy.
Benefits: Decarbonization, energy security, industrial feedstock.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the technological and economic challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption across various sectors in India.
Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors and contribute to India's net-zero targets.
Examine the policy support and infrastructure development required to establish India as a global leader in the hydrogen economy.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, emitting no greenhouse gases.

GS3.headline: Government Constitutes High-Level Task Force to Review India's Cyber Security Framework
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) establishes a high-level task force to conduct a comprehensive review of India's cybersecurity framework.
• The task force is mandated to address evolving cyber threats, including state-sponsored attacks and cyber warfare.
• Key recommendations focus on strengthening critical information infrastructure protection and capacity building.
• Emphasizes international cooperation and public-private partnerships to enhance national cyber resilience.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Task Force: High-level for cybersecurity review.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA).
Focus: Evolving cyber threats, critical infrastructure protection, capacity building.
Recommendations: International cooperation, public-private partnerships.
Related agency: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team).
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges posed by evolving cyber threats to India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key recommendations of the task force and their potential impact on strengthening India's cybersecurity posture.
Examine the role of international cooperation and public-private partnerships in building a robust and resilient cybersecurity framework.
Evaluate the importance of a comprehensive national cybersecurity strategy in protecting critical information infrastructure and citizen data.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital shield icon overlaying a network of interconnected lines, symbolizing cybersecurity protection.
GS3.trivia: India's Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) is the national agency for handling cyber security incidents.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs Releases Urban Mobility Index 2024, Highlighting Key Performance Areas
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveils the Urban Mobility Index (UMI) 2024 for Indian cities.
• The index evaluates cities based on parameters such as public transport availability, infrastructure quality, and sustainability.
• Aims to benchmark urban mobility performance, identify areas for improvement, and promote best practices.
• Encourages cities to adopt smart mobility solutions and integrate transport planning with urban development.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Index Name: Urban Mobility Index (UMI) 2024.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Parameters: Public transport, infrastructure, sustainability, accessibility.
Objective: Benchmarking, promoting best practices, smart mobility.
Significance: Guides urban transport planning.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical challenges faced by urban transport systems in India's rapidly growing cities.
Analyze how an Urban Mobility Index can serve as a tool for improving urban planning and promoting sustainable transport solutions.
Examine the role of technology and smart city initiatives in enhancing urban mobility and last-mile connectivity.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits of efficient and accessible public transport for urban populations.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized city skyline with various modes of transport (bus, metro, bicycle) moving efficiently.
GS3.trivia: Delhi Metro, one of the largest and busiest metro networks in the world, is a prime example of a successful urban mobility project in India.

GS3.headline: Progress on National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems Reviewed
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology reviews the progress of the National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS).
• The mission focuses on developing technologies like AI, IoT, Robotics, and Quantum Computing.
• Aims to create a strong ecosystem for R&D and human resource development in CPS technologies.
• Crucial for India's technological self-reliance and competitiveness in the Fourth Industrial Revolution.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Mission Name: National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS).
Nodal Department: Department of Science & Technology (DST).
Technologies covered: AI, IoT, Robotics, Quantum Computing, Big Data Analytics.
Objective: R&D, human resource development, technological self-reliance.
Significance: Aligns with Industry 4.0.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) in driving India's economic growth and societal development.
Analyze the role of the National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS) in fostering indigenous innovation and human resource capabilities.
Examine the ethical and governance challenges associated with the widespread adoption of AI, IoT, and robotics in various sectors.
Evaluate how India can leverage CPS technologies to enhance its competitiveness in the global technological landscape and prepare for the Fourth Industrial Revolution.
GS3.imageDescription: A futuristic cityscape with interconnected smart devices and robots, representing Cyber-Physical Systems.
GS3.trivia: Cyber-Physical Systems are integrations of computation, networking, and physical processes, often seen in smart grids, autonomous vehicles, and advanced manufacturing.

sources: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
